message:
chore(beads): update issue bd-19kr status to in_progress for deprecated file removal

Mark the "Remove deprecated collect/repro_bug.rs" issue as in_progress
and refresh its timestamp to reflect active work on the cleanup task.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-14wg","title":"Add CommandPalette for fuzzy command search","description":"# Add CommandPalette Widget

## What
Integrate ftui's CommandPalette widget to provide a fuzzy-searchable command interface
(Ctrl+P or ':' trigger). This is a significant UX upgrade over the current help overlay.

## ftui CommandPalette
ftui provides a built-in CommandPalette with:
- Fuzzy search with Bayesian scoring (log Bayes factors + evidence ledger)
- Ranked command list with match highlighting
- Keyboard shortcut display alongside each command
- Customizable command registry
- Fast incremental search

## Commands to Register
- Navigation: "Go to first process", "Go to last process", "Page down", etc.
- Selection: "Select all", "Deselect all", "Select recommended", "Invert selection"
- Views: "Show summary", "Show genealogy", "Show galaxy brain", "Toggle detail"
- Actions: "Execute selected", "Refresh processes", "Toggle goal view"
- Settings: "Switch to dark theme", "Switch to light theme"
- Help: "Show keyboard shortcuts"

## Trigger
- Ctrl+P (standard palette trigger)
- ':' (vim-style command mode)

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/app.rs` — add CommandPalette state and rendering
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/msg.rs` — add palette-related messages

## Acceptance Criteria
- Ctrl+P opens command palette overlay
- Fuzzy search filters commands as you type
- Selecting a command triggers the corresponding action
- Keyboard shortcuts shown next to each command
- Esc dismisses palette","notes":"Alien uplift: command palette ranking via Bayesian score (query match + recency/use priors) with deterministic lexical fallback; include latency budget + keyflow property tests.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:16:01.949573781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:13:27.643162654Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T23:13:27.643137597Z","close_reason":"Implemented CommandPalette integration (Ctrl+P/: triggers, fuzzy action dispatch, overlay render, tests). Full pt-core check remains blocked by pre-existing replay compile errors unrelated to this bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["enhancement","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14wg","depends_on_id":"bd-1enw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-14wg","depends_on_id":"bd-251t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:36.088028334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-14wg","depends_on_id":"bd-3hpi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-14wg","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1591","title":"No-mock capabilities + environment detection tests","description":"## Goal
Cover capabilities/environment detection with real code paths (no mocks), with E2E coverage provided in bd-3t22.

## Scope
- Unit tests for capability parsing + validation (OS/arch/paths/privileges/tools).
- Integration tests for capability manifest generation + cache read/write.
- Recorded capability fixtures from real hosts (sanitized/redacted).
- Schema validation for capability JSON outputs.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs per fixture: event, timestamp, host_id, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Store fixtures under `test/fixtures/capabilities/` with checksums + manifest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit coverage for parsers/validators without mocks.
- [ ] Integration coverage for manifest generation + caching.
- [ ] Schema validation passes on all fixtures.
- [ ] Fixtures + logs are consumable by bd-3t22 E2E script.

## Test Plan
- Unit: parser + validator for capability structs.
- Integration: generate manifest, read back, validate schema + redaction.

 {"id":"bd-1b8k","title":"E2E: policy guardrails (rate limit, blast radius, max kills)","description":"## Goal
Validate safety guardrails end-to-end with detailed JSONL logging.

## Scope
- Rate limiting: per-minute/hour/session counters, cooldown warnings, and hard blocks.
- Blast radius warnings: ensure high-impact actions require explicit confirmation paths.
- Max kills per session: verify guardrail enforcement in plan/apply.
- Negative paths: malformed policy.json, missing policy file, override attempts logged.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing error messages are clear and actionable.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs: event, cmd, guardrail_type, threshold, observed, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: plan outputs, apply outputs (dry-run), stderr snapshots, manifest.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Guardrails are enforced in plan/apply flows.
- [ ] Warnings and blocks are surfaced clearly to users.
- [ ] Override attempts are logged with explicit user intent markers.
- [ ] Error paths produce correct exit codes and guidance.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate.

## Test Plan
- E2E script using shared harness + fixtures: run `pt agent plan/apply --dry-run` against crafted inputs.

 {"id":"bd-1enw","title":"Port keyboard bindings and event dispatch to ftui Message pattern","description":"# Port Keyboard Bindings and Event Dispatch

## What
Migrate the keyboard binding system (tui/events.rs, 203 lines) from direct crossterm
event matching to ftui's Event → Message conversion pattern. This is the bridge
between raw terminal input and the Elm update() cycle.

## Current KeyBindings System (events.rs)
```rust
pub struct KeyBindings {
    pub quit: Vec<KeyEvent>,
    pub cursor_up: Vec<KeyEvent>,
    pub cursor_down: Vec<KeyEvent>,
    // ... 20+ binding groups
}
impl Default for KeyBindings {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            quit: vec\![key('q'), key_esc()],
            cursor_up: vec\![key('k'), key_up()],
            cursor_down: vec\![key('j'), key_down()],
            // ...
        }
    }
}
```

Event handling is in `app.rs` as a big match chain:
```rust
fn handle_key_event(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> Result<()> {
    match self.state {
        AppState::Normal => self.handle_normal_key(key),
        AppState::Searching => self.handle_search_key(key),
        AppState::Confirming => self.handle_confirm_key(key),
        AppState::Help => self.handle_help_key(key),
    }
}
```

## ftui Event Pattern

### Step 1: From<Event> for Msg
Convert raw ftui Events into domain Messages:
```rust
impl From<ftui::Event> for Msg {
    fn from(event: ftui::Event) -> Self {
        match event {
            ftui::Event::Key(key) => Msg::KeyPressed(key),
            ftui::Event::Resize { width, height } => Msg::Resized { width, height },
            ftui::Event::Tick => Msg::Tick,
            _ => Msg::Noop,
        }
    }
}
```

### Step 2: Key dispatch in update()
```rust
fn update(&mut self, msg: Msg) -> Cmd<Msg> {
    match msg {
        Msg::KeyPressed(key) => {
            // Structured tracing for input debugging
            tracing::debug\!(
                target: "tui.user_input",
                key_code = ?key.code,
                modifiers = ?key.modifiers,
                app_state = ?self.state,
                focus = ?self.focus,
                "Key event received"
            );
            self.handle_key(key)
        }
        Msg::Resized { width, height } => {
            tracing::debug\!(
                target: "tui.state_transition",
                from_breakpoint = ?self.layout_state.breakpoint(),
                width, height,
                "Terminal resized"
            );
            self.layout_state.update(width, height);
            Cmd::none()
        }
        // ...
    }
}

fn handle_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> Cmd<Msg> {
    // State-dependent dispatch (same pattern as current, but returns Cmd)
    match self.state {
        AppState::Normal => self.handle_normal_key(key),
        AppState::Searching => self.handle_search_key(key),
        AppState::Confirming => self.handle_confirm_key(key),
        AppState::Help => self.handle_help_key(key),
    }
}
```

### Step 3: Key mapping
Map ftui KeyCode/Modifiers to actions. ftui uses the same KeyCode enum as crossterm
(it's derived from crossterm internally), so most key patterns transfer directly:
- `KeyCode::Char('j')` → CursorDown
- `KeyCode::Char('q')` → Quit
- `KeyCode::Char('/')` → EnterSearchMode
- etc.

## ftui Key Types
```rust
ftui::KeyEvent { code: KeyCode, modifiers: Modifiers, kind: KeyEventKind }
ftui::KeyCode::Char('c'), Enter, Esc, Up, Down, Left, Right, Home, End, PageUp, PageDown,
               Tab, BackTab, F(1-12), Delete, Backspace, Insert, Null
ftui::Modifiers::CTRL | ALT | SHIFT | SUPER  (bitflags)
```

## State-Dependent Key Handling
Key behavior changes based on AppState. The match structure remains similar:
- **Normal**: Navigation, selection, view switching, action triggers
- **Searching**: Text input, history navigation, commit/cancel
- **Confirming**: Tab between buttons, Enter/y/n, Esc cancel
- **Help**: Any key or Esc dismisses

## Important: Return Cmd, Not Mutate-and-Return
Every key handler returns `Cmd<Msg>`:
- Most return `Cmd::none()` (just state mutation)
- 'r' returns `Cmd::perform(async_scan, Msg::RefreshComplete)`
- 'e' + confirm returns `Cmd::perform(async_execute, Msg::ExecutionComplete)`
- 'q' returns `Cmd::quit()`

---

## Structured Logging for Key Events

Every key press must be traced at DEBUG level for debugging. This is critical for
diagnosing user-reported issues ("I pressed X and nothing happened"):

```rust
fn handle_normal_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> Cmd<Msg> {
    match key.code {
        KeyCode::Char('j') | KeyCode::Down => {
            self.process_table.next();
            tracing::trace\!(target: "tui.user_input", action = "cursor_down", "Navigation");
            Cmd::none()
        }
        KeyCode::Char('e') => {
            tracing::info\!(target: "tui.user_input", action = "request_execute",
                selected_count = self.process_table.selected_count(), "Execute requested");
            self.state = AppState::Confirming;
            Cmd::none()
        }
        KeyCode::Char('q') => {
            tracing::info\!(target: "tui.user_input", action = "quit", "Quit requested");
            Cmd::quit()
        }
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Log Level Guidelines for Key Handlers
- Navigation (j/k/arrows): TRACE (very frequent, only needed for deep debugging)
- Mode changes (search, confirm, help): DEBUG
- Actions (execute, refresh): INFO (user-initiated operations)
- Quit: INFO

---

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/events.rs` — adapt key binding definitions for ftui KeyCode
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/app.rs` — key dispatch in update() with tracing

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing key bindings preserved (vim-style + arrows)
- State-dependent dispatch works for all 4 states
- Each handler returns appropriate Cmd (none/perform/quit)
- No raw crossterm event types in TUI code
- Every key press logged at appropriate tracing level
- State transitions logged at DEBUG level","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:12:44.432356061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:38:48.777033290Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T04:38:48.776960605Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["events","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1enw","depends_on_id":"bd-3hpi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1enw","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1enw","depends_on_id":"bd-iykp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gut","title":"Port TUI integration tests to ftui Model","description":"# Port TUI Integration Tests

## What
Migrate the TUI integration tests (crates/pt-core/tests/tui_integration.rs, ~83 lines)
from ratatui's TestBackend to ftui's test infrastructure.

## Current Integration Tests
- Galaxy brain split view rendering
- Help overlay rendering
- App-level state transitions
- Uses ratatui::Terminal<TestBackend> for rendering

## ftui Test Pattern
In ftui, integration tests can:
1. Create a Model instance
2. Send Messages via update()
3. Assert state changes
4. Render into TestTerminal and assert visual output

```rust
#[test]
fn help_overlay_shows_on_question_mark() {
    let mut app = create_test_app();
    assert_eq!(app.state, AppState::Normal);
    
    app.update(Msg::ToggleHelp);
    assert_eq!(app.state, AppState::Help);
    
    let mut term = TestTerminal::new(120, 40);
    term.render(|frame| app.view(frame));
    // Assert help content is visible in buffer
}
```

## Key Difference
Current tests render via `terminal.draw(|f| app.render(f))` — in ftui, tests use
`model.view(&mut frame)` directly. The rendering path is the same but the API differs.

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/tests/tui_integration.rs` — rewrite for ftui

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing integration test scenarios covered
- State transitions verified (Normal → Help, Normal → Searching, etc.)
- Visual assertions pass (help overlay visible, detail pane toggles, etc.)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:26.141532705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:49:00.674944631Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:49:00.674880011Z","close_reason":"Absorbed into bd-36a2 (Port TUI golden, snapshot, and integration tests). The golden and integration test beads were merged into a single comprehensive testing bead because they share test infrastructure (ftui-harness TestTerminal), depend on the same widget implementations, and benefit from shared test helpers.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["test","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gut","depends_on_id":"bd-2b3l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1gut","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
